novel_features.name: Subscription Detective
novel_features.description: Find regular-cadence merchant charges that are not yet linked to a recurring item, so you can promote them before subscription creep sneaks past you.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local self-join across transactions detecting monthly cadence (3+ occurrences, ±3 day jitter, amount ±10%) — no Lunch Money endpoint exposes this view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a user asks 'what subscriptions are draining my account?', this surfaces unlabeled recurring charges in one local SQL pass.

novel_features.name: Recurring Miss Audit
novel_features.description: List recurring items whose next expected date falls in the requested month but have no matching transaction yet, so you can catch missed bills.
novel_features.rationale: Left-joins local recurring_items against transactions on payee + amount band — Lunch Money's API returns each list separately and never the gap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'which subscriptions haven't hit my account yet?' which is a top-three Maya question with no API equivalent.

novel_features.name: Duplicate Charge Finder
novel_features.description: Detect near-duplicate transactions in a window (same merchant, amount within tolerance, date within range) so you can group or delete them in bulk.
novel_features.rationale: Self-join on the local transactions table — no Lunch Money endpoint surfaces dup candidates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use after a trip or when reviewing imports to catch the same Uber ride processed twice.

novel_features.name: Stale Balance Audit
novel_features.description: Surface manual accounts and manual crypto whose balance has not been updated in N days, so net worth stays honest.
novel_features.rationale: UNIONs manual_accounts.last_updated, crypto_manual.last_updated, and the tail of balance_history per account — three local tables, one query, no API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'what manual balances do I owe an update?' before quarterly NAV updates or Sunday reconciliation.

novel_features.name: Net Worth At Date
novel_features.description: Reconstruct net worth at any historical date by walking balance_history across every account type, with last-known-balance carry-forward where needed.
novel_features.rationale: Picks the latest balance_history row at-or-before the date per account; sums per account-type — local SQL only, no API equivalent.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for quarterly snapshots, retirement-plan inputs, or 'what was my net worth before I bought the car?' questions.

novel_features.name: Bulk Smart Retag
novel_features.description: Match transactions by regex on payee/notes via local FTS, preview the affected set, then bulk-apply tag/category changes via the API.
novel_features.rationale: Local FTS5 finds the candidate set, --dry-run shows count + samples, then a single bulk PUT /transactions applies the change — the web UI breaks past ~20 rows.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'fix every Amazon transaction since January' in one command instead of pagination clicks.

novel_features.name: Uncategorized Triage Inbox
novel_features.description: Pull all unreviewed transactions, compute a suggested category from prior categorizations of the same merchant, and optionally mass-apply the suggestion.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local unreviewed transactions against the historical categorization frequency per payee — no API can suggest categories from your own past patterns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Sunday-morning ritual for power-user budgeters; agent-friendly suggested categories let an LLM bulk-approve high-confidence rows.

novel_features.name: Budget Burn-Down
novel_features.description: For the current budget period, project end-of-month spend per category at the current linear rate and flag categories projected to overshoot.
novel_features.rationale: Joins budgets.settings × categories × transactions locally, computes days_remaining and projected_end_spend — Lunch Money's web UI shows a chart but no CLI pipes it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Mid-month answer to 'am I on track?' that flows into agent workflows and dashboards.

narrative.headline: A Go CLI for Lunch Money's official v2 OpenAPI — offline SQLite store, subscription detection, and bulk smart retag the web UI cannot do.
narrative.value_prop: Built on Lunch Money's published v2 alpha spec for forward compatibility — every endpoint exposed as a Cobra subcommand with --json, --select dotted-paths, --csv, --dry-run, and typed exit codes. Local SQLite store powers offline search, history queries, and joins (net worth at any date, stale balance audit, duplicate detection, subscription detective) that single API calls can't answer. A companion `lunch-money-pp-mcp` binary ships alongside for Claude Desktop / Claude Code MCP integration.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authenticate with a bearer token from https://my.lunchmoney.app/developers. The CLI accepts the token via five env-var aliases — LUNCHMONEY_ACCESS_TOKEN (canonical, used by the official docs and the Python lunchable client), plus LUNCHMONEY_API_KEY, LUNCHMONEY_TOKEN, LUNCHMONEY_API_TOKEN (lunchtui), and LUNCH_MONEY_API_KEY (robshox MCP) — to match whichever spelling your ecosystem already uses. The same token works against the live API at api.lunchmoney.dev/v2 and the mock server at mock.lunchmoney.dev/v2.

quickstart.comment: Paste your token from my.lunchmoney.app/developers; the CLI writes it to config and points doctor at the live API.
Confirm the token is valid, the API is reachable, and the local store is initialized — before any sync.
Full pull of categories, tags, accounts, recurring items, and transactions into the local SQLite store with rate-limit-aware backoff.
See unreviewed transactions with suggested categories computed from your own historical categorizations.
Preview a bulk retag before letting the bulk PUT /transactions endpoint apply it.
Mid-month per-category burn-down with end-of-period projection — pipe into an agent or dashboard.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `lunch-money-pp-cli auth status` to see which env var is in scope; if none, paste your token via `lunch-money-pp-cli auth set-token` or export LUNCHMONEY_ACCESS_TOKEN.
Built-in backoff respects x-ratelimit-remaining and x-ratelimit-reset; if you hit a hard ceiling, reduce --limit on transactions list or stagger sync calls.
Run `lunch-money-pp-cli sync` to pull only changes since last cursor (uses updated_since on transactions); pass --full to rebuild from scratch.
Run `lunch-money-pp-cli plaid-accounts trigger-fetch` to trigger the Lunch Money backend Plaid fetch; rerun sync after a minute to pull new transactions.
The CLI picks LUNCHMONEY_ACCESS_TOKEN first, then the four aliases in fallback order; `auth status` prints which was used.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need to bulk-fix transactions, audit subscriptions or recurring items, reconcile net worth across Plaid + manual + crypto accounts, or expose Lunch Money to agent workflows. Especially valuable for Sunday-morning budgeting rituals (triage, retag, budget burn-down), end-of-month asset snapshots (manual account updates, balance history upserts), and expense-report assembly (transactions list --tag, attachment exports). Avoid it for one-off web-UI exploration — that's what lunchmoney.app is for.

recipes.title: Sunday triage with agent-applied suggestions
recipes.explanation: Pipe the high-confidence triage rows to jq for an ID list, then apply them via `transactions retag --ids "$IDS" --category-id <id>` (which calls the bulk PUT /transactions endpoint under the hood).

recipes.title: Find the asset driving this month's drift
recipes.command: lunch-money-pp-cli net-worth on 2026-04-30 --json --select 'by_account_type.account_name,by_account_type.balance' && lunch-money-pp-cli net-worth on 2026-05-12 --json --select 'by_account_type.account_name,by_account_type.balance'
recipes.explanation: Run net-worth at two dates and diff the per-account balances — answers Devon's Sunday 'what moved?' question without exporting CSV.

recipes.title: Promote a stealth subscription to a recurring item
recipes.explanation: Surfaces local-detected subscription patterns (≥3 occurrences, monthly cadence) not yet flagged. Filter with jq for subscriptions over a threshold; the v2 API does not yet expose a recurring_items create endpoint, so promote each one in the web UI at https://my.lunchmoney.app/recurring.

recipes.title: Drill into a budget category with deeply nested occurrences
recipes.explanation: The summary endpoint returns category objects with nested occurrences arrays. Dotted --select narrows the payload to the three fields needed for a per-month spend-vs-budget table without parsing the rest.

recipes.title: Bulk retag every Amazon transaction since January
recipes.explanation: Dry-run shows count + sample rows from the local FTS5 search. Drop --dry-run to apply via the bulk PUT /transactions endpoint.
